home.introduction: ProcessPuzzle/... is a set of libraries to easy building of an Angular application. It helps with very low level tasks like application initializing or **Low-Code** plattform for generating tables and CRUD forms run-time from entity descriptors.
home.utils_card_content: This is a collection of functions, services and components useful when building Angular applications. The package contains:
home.utils_card_content_1: runtime-configuration - reads the application runtime, at app initialisation
home.utils_card_content_2: app-initializer - service which helps by application initialization
home.utils_card_content_3: layout-service - to help by responsive layouts
home.widgets_card_content: This is a collection of UI components useful when building Angular UI. The package contains:
home.widgets_card_content_1: Back Button - navigates back to the previous page
home.widgets_card_content_2: Like Button - enables to count fans for the page
home.widgets_card_content_3: Share Button - facilitates to share a link to the application with others
home.auth_card_content: This is a collection of UI components and services for authentication and authorisation. The package contains:
home.auth_card_content_1: Auth Button - menu for authentication actions
home.auth_card_content_4: Registration Component - facilitates user to register
home.auth_card_content_5: My Profile Component - enables to review and change user profile data
home.auth_card_content_6: Auth Service - helps to authenticate users
home.auth_card_content_7: Auth Guard - support authorisation true routes
home.base-entity_card_subtitle: Base classes for reactive forms and material tables
home.base-entity_card_content: Facilitates of building full functional Forms and Tables with connection to data source and support of Signal Store stage management from simple Entity Definition.
home.base-entity_card_content_1: Generate Angular Material Table with filter, search and paging capabilities
home.base-entity_card_content_2: Generate full flagged reactive form with checks and error messages
home.base-entity_card_content_3: Generate state dependent header to navigate from table to form and back
home.base-rule_card_subtitle: Declarative business rules at design and run-time
home.base-rule_card_content: Provides a uniform way to define, import, export and evaluate business rules over your domain objects without scattering conditions through the code base.
home.base-rule_card_content_1: Define rules in YAML and load them as RuleDefinitions
home.base-rule_card_content_2: Evaluate objects against rule sets and collect violations
home.base-rule_card_content_3: Import and export rule catalogues between environments
home.test-utils_card_subtitle: Utilities for unit- and integration testing
home.test-utils_card_content: This is a collection of functions and services to easy unit- and integration testing.
home.test-utils_card_content_1: mock-breakpoint-observer - help to test responsive pages. Imitates resizing of a page.
home.test-utils_card_content_2: transloco-testing-module - sets up Transloco for testing Component with i18n capabilities.
home.test-utils_card_content_3: layout-service - to help by responsive layouts
home.ci-cd_card_subtitle: GitHub Actions base continuous delivery process
home.ci-cd_card_content:
This isn't a library, but an end-to-end solutions for continuos delivery pipeline, base on GitHub Actions.
            It supports multiple quality assurance practices und multiple development stages. At the end delivers increments automatically to production.
home.ci-cd_card_content_2: Supports deploy to AWS or Firebase
home.ci-cd_card_content_3: Supports to publish to NPM registry

base-entity.samples_desc_2: The tables and forms below are generated from the entities end their attribute descriptors, run-time.

base-rules.samples_desc_1: The following sample entities demonstrate the capabilities of the rule engine:
base-rules.samples_desc_2: Pick a sample below to explore how rules are defined and evaluated against it.
base-rules.intro_paragraph_1: The ProcessPuzzle Rule Engine lets Low-Code applications author, evaluate and visualize business rules. It combines a set of Angular building blocks for the user interface with a Spring Boot backend that stores and executes the rule definitions.
base-rules.intro_paragraph_2: The engine is split into two complementary libraries which share a common domain vocabulary:
base-rules.intro_frontend_desc: Angular services, stores and components used to design, edit and visualize rules in the browser.
base-rules.intro_backend_desc: Spring Boot module that persists rule definitions and evaluates them on the server side, following the platform's Hexagonal architecture.
